records.ety: [L. cancellare to make like a lattice, to strike or cross out (cf. Fr. canceller, OF. canceler) fr. cancelli lattice, crossbars, dim. of cancer lattice; cf. Gr. <?/ latticed gate. Cf. Chancel.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Canceled or Cancelled (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Canceling or Cancelling.]

defs: To inclose or surround, as with a railing, or with latticework.
quotes: A little obscure place canceled in with iron work is the pillar or stump at which . . . our Savior was scourged.

defs: To shut out, as with a railing or with latticework; to exclude.
To cross and deface, as the lines of a writing, or as a word or figure; to mark out by a cross line; to blot out or obliterate.
quotes: A deed may be avoided by delivering it up to be cancelled; that is, to have lines drawn over it in the form of latticework or cancelli; though the phrase is now used figuratively for any manner of obliterating or defacing it.

defs: To annul or destroy; to revoke or recall.
quotes: He was unwilling to cancel the interest created through former secret services, by being refractory on this occasion.

defs: To suppress or omit; to strike out, as matter in type.

records.ety: [See Cancel, v. i., and cf. Chancel.]

defs: An inclosure; a boundary; a limit.
quotes: A prison is but a retirement, and opportunity of serious thoughts, to a person whose spirit . . . desires no enlargement beyond the cancels of the body.

defs: The suppression or striking out of matter in type, or of a printed page or pages.
